menu_options.serial: Direct RS232 serial cable to this machine

serial.title: Direct RS232 serial connection
serial.description: Pick the serial port the projector is (or will be) plugged into. If it isn't connected right now, you can type a path manually.

confirm_model.title: Confirm projector model
confirm_model.description: {detected} Pick the matching profile below, or override it if it guessed wrong. Compare the picture in the documentation against your unit if you're not sure.

menu_options.show_test_pattern: Show test pattern on the projector

error.cannot_resolve_host: Could not resolve that hostname. Double-check the spelling, or use the projector's IP address instead.
error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the projector. Check the host/port, that the projector is powered on, and that Network Function + Telnet are enabled in its on-screen menu.
error.cannot_open_serial: Could not open that serial port. Check the path/permissions, and that nothing else has it open.
error.invalid_mac: That doesn't look like a valid MAC address (expected something like AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF).
error.unknown: Unexpected error while connecting to the projector.

send_command.name: Send raw RS232 command
send_command.description: Send a raw RS232 command code to the projector and return its reply.

code.name: Command code
code.description: The numeric command code from Optoma's RS232 table.

value.name: Value
value.description: The value to send after the code (omit for value-less commands).

entry_id.name: Device
entry_id.description: Config entry ID to target if you have more than one projector configured.

set_test_pattern.name: Show or hide test pattern
set_test_pattern.description: Toggles the projector's built-in test pattern, if its profile supports one.

enabled.name: Enabled
enabled.description: True to show the test pattern, false to hide it.

entry_id.name: Device
entry_id.description: Config entry ID to target if you have more than one projector configured.
